summary: schema-guard is a published, installable runx skill (epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7) that checks a proposed JSON Schema against the current one for breaking changes, validates sample payloads, and emits a gated publish_schema_proposal for the schema-publisher executor only when the change is compatible — never writing a live schema. Local harness passed 3/3; the post-publish dogfood sealed receipt runx:receipt:sha256:092aa5a22343fe685440a75dcdcb7c1d50d5253016ccc81ebe5fa3aa82b2da87 with compatible=false (block=true), and runx verify returned valid.
observations: runx CLI version: runx-cli 0.6.14 (publish, install, dogfood, and verify were all run with this binary).
Publisher owner: epistemedeus. Package name: schema-guard (exact). Version: 0.1.0.
Registry ref: epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7. Public adoption page (public_url): https://runx.ai/x/epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7.
Source/provenance (source_url): https://github.com/epistemedeus/schema-guard/tree/27f6eff6ae3168d1fb43efe586d02afabb3bbcbf.
Pull request against runxhq/runx (pr_url): https://github.com/runxhq/runx/pull/238. Head commit: 9c2f23aca50722af760b0e7a5eda1cba1774fc34.
Raw X.yaml from the PR head commit: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/epistemedeus/runx/9c2f23aca50722af760b0e7a5eda1cba1774fc34/skills/schema-guard/X.yaml.
Raw SKILL.md from the PR head commit: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/epistemedeus/runx/9c2f23aca50722af760b0e7a5eda1cba1774fc34/skills/schema-guard/SKILL.md.
Publish method: runx login --provider github --for publish; runx registry publish ./skills/schema-guard/SKILL.md --registry https://api.runx.ai.
Install command: runx add epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7 --registry https://api.runx.ai.

Local harness (pre-publish): status=passed (3/3, 0 assertion errors, with packet assertions), cases=[additive-change-compatible-yields-proposal:sealed, breaking-change-withholds-proposal:sealed, refuses-to-apply-schema:failure].
Hosted registry harness (post-publish): green — the registry publish gate ran the hosted harness including the refuses-to-apply-schema stop case.

Dogfood command: runx skill epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7 --registry https://api.runx.ai --input-json current_schema='...' --input-json proposed_schema='...' --input-json sample_payloads='...' --input-json compatibility_policy='...' --receipt-dir <dir> -j.
Dogfood receipt_ref (post-publish run of epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7, not a harness fixture seal): runx:receipt:sha256:092aa5a22343fe685440a75dcdcb7c1d50d5253016ccc81ebe5fa3aa82b2da87.
runx verify verdict on the dogfood receipt: valid.
Compatibility status on the dogfood input: compatible=false, block=true, proposal_status=withheld.
Breaking changes detected: id: type_changed (integer->string); name: field_removed.
Sample validation_results: 1 sample validated, 1 failed against the proposed schema.
The publish_schema_proposal is gated: performed_by=schema-publisher, requires_approval=true; schema-guard writes no live schema.
Compatible harness case 'additive-change-compatible-yields-proposal' seals with a proposal; the breaking case withholds the proposal; the apply case is the governed stop.

New-user path: install with 'runx add epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7 --registry https://api.runx.ai', run 'runx skill epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7 --registry https://api.runx.ai --input-json current_schema='...' --input-json proposed_schema='...' --input-json sample_payloads='...' --input-json compatibility_policy='...' --receipt-dir <dir> -j', and verify the sealed receipt with 'runx verify --receipt receipt.json --json'. verification.json records the public key so verification needs no private context.

dogfood.input: current_schema requires {id:int, name:string}; proposed_schema drops 'name' and retypes id to string; policy pins required_fields [id, name]
dogfood.command: runx skill epistemedeus/schema-guard@sha-519d004c03f7 --registry https://api.runx.ai --input-json current_schema='...' --input-json proposed_schema='...' --input-json sample_payloads='...' --input-json compatibility_policy='...' --receipt-dir <dir> -j
